league.name: La Liga
league.description: Spain’s top flight: technical football, strong youth academies, and two global giants in Real Madrid and Barcelona. Smaller clubs and regions (Basque Country, Seville, Valencia) add identity beyond the big two.
league.styleOfPlay: Traditionally more possession and touch in midfield; patience to find gaps. Defending is organised; attackers use dribbling and combinations. Real and Barça set the star standard, while others often defend deep and counter.
famousClubs: Real Madrid — European Cup record and galáctico eras
Barcelona — La Masia, tiki-taka, Catalan identity
Atlético Madrid — gritty defending and Diego Simeone style
Real Sociedad — Basque academy and possession football
famousPlayers: Vinícius Júnior — Real Madrid left winger
Jude Bellingham — Real Madrid midfielder and goal threat
Pedri — Barcelona midfielder, Spain youth graduate
league.historySummary: La Liga grew through the 20th century with Madrid and Barça at the centre. Spanish talent pools fed midfield artists and smart defenders. The league exported coaches and style worldwide, even as the big two dominate headlines and TV interest.
rivalries: El Clásico — Real Madrid vs Barcelona
Madrid derby — Real Madrid vs Atlético Madrid
Seville derby — Sevilla vs Real Betis
league.fanGuide: Start with El Clásico to see Spain’s biggest story. Notice how Barça fans tie club to Catalan culture; Madrid fans expect European nights. Late kickoff times (UK viewers) and strong regional derbies help you see beyond two clubs.

teams.name: Real Madrid
teams.shortHistory: Real Madrid measure success in European Cups — they have won the competition more than any other club. From Di Stéfano to the galácticos and modern stars, the story is star power, late goals, and pressure to win every season.
teams.fanGuide: White shirts at the Santiago Bernabéu; “Hala Madrid” is the chant to know. El Clásico vs Barcelona is the world’s most watched club fixture. The Madrid derby vs Atlético splits the capital. Expect talk of legends, transfers, and whether the team “knows how to win” in big moments.
legends: Alfredo Di Stéfano — forward who shaped the club’s first European dominance
Raúl — one-club striker; symbol of loyalty in white before the galácticos
Cristiano Ronaldo — record goalscorer in Madrid; four Champions Leagues there
Zinedine Zidane — playmaker turned manager; three straight European Cups as coach
currentKeyPlayers: Vinícius Júnior — left wing; dribbling and big-game goals
Jude Bellingham — midfielder; arrives in the box and leads by example
Federico Valverde — engine; covers ground in midfield or wide
Luka Modrić — veteran playmaker; outside-foot passes and control

teams.name: Barcelona
teams.shortHistory: Barcelona’s identity is La Masia — the academy that taught possession football to the world. The Messi era brought endless trophies; recent years mixed financial trouble with rebuilds built on Pedri, Gavi, and Catalan pride.
teams.fanGuide: Blaugrana stripes (blue and garnet); supporters call themselves culers. “Més que un club” means the team stands for Catalan identity as well as football. El Clásico vs Real Madrid is the must-learn fixture; the city derby vs Espanyol is smaller but fierce. “Cant del Barça” often plays after home wins.
legends: Lionel Messi — greatest player in club history; hundreds of goals in blaugrana
Xavi — midfield metronome; defined tiki-taka as player and coach
Andrés Iniesta — clutch creator; World Cup winner and fan favourite
Carles Puyol — captain; warrior centre-back of the 2000s–2010s
currentKeyPlayers: Robert Lewandowski — striker; movement and finishing in the box
Pedri — midfielder; press resistance and passing
Gavi — midfielder; energy, tackling, and bite
Raphinha — right wing; dribbling and direct runs

teams.name: Atlético Madrid
teams.shortHistory: Atlético built a reputation as the capital’s gritty alternative to Real’s glamour — Simeone’s era added titles through organisation, set pieces, and fan intensity.
teams.fanGuide: Red and white stripes; the Madrid derby splits the city. “El Cholo” chants reference long-running manager Diego Simeone.
legends: Luis Aragonés — player and title-winning coach
Diego Godín — defensive rock of title years
currentKeyPlayers: Antoine Griezmann — forward and creative hub

teams.name: Sevilla
teams.shortHistory: Sevilla became Europe’s specialist in the Europa League, winning the competition repeatedly while the big two dominated La Liga headlines.
teams.fanGuide: White shirts with red accents; the Seville derby vs Betis is one of Spain’s fiercest local rivalries.
legends: Diego Maradona — brief but iconic spell
Iván Rakitić — midfield engine of trophy eras

teams.name: Athletic Club
teams.shortHistory: Athletic Club operate a unique policy of fielding only players from the Basque Country. Despite this restriction they have never been relegated from La Liga, hold a historic relationship with their region, and have won eight league titles.
teams.fanGuide: San Mamés — nicknamed the Cathedral — is one of Europe's iconic football cathedrals. The Basque-only policy and their unique identity make Athletic Club genuinely one-of-a-kind.

teams.name: Real Sociedad
teams.shortHistory: San Sebastián club who won back-to-back La Liga titles in 1981 and 1982 and are known for developing Basque talent alongside foreign stars. Their modern era saw them qualify regularly for European competition.
teams.fanGuide: The Basque derby against Athletic Club — txuri-urdin against the reds and whites — is the Basque Country's defining football match.

teams.name: Real Betis
teams.shortHistory: Seville's green-and-white half, Betis won their only league title in 1935 and have enjoyed periodic revivals with exciting attacking football. The Seville derby against FC Sevilla is one of Spain's most atmospheric local derbies.
teams.fanGuide: Béticos are known for their passion and willingness to applaud good football, even from opponents. The derby against Sevilla FC fills the whole city.

teams.name: Villarreal
teams.shortHistory: The Yellow Submarine from a small town in Valencia province have become a consistent La Liga presence and Europa League winners in 2021 under Unai Emery, defeating Manchester United on penalties in the final.
teams.fanGuide: Villarreal is a small-town club that over-achieves through smart management and a clear footballing identity. Their Europa League win is the high-water mark of a remarkable rise.

teams.name: Celta Vigo
teams.shortHistory: Galician club from the far northwest corner of Spain, Celta Vigo are known for technical, attacking football shaped by their history of producing creative players from the region.
teams.fanGuide: The Galician derby against Deportivo is the local rivalry. Celta's sky-blue and white shirts and their Balaídos ground are the symbols of football in Galicia.

teams.name: Valencia
teams.shortHistory: Valencia were the dominant force in Spanish football at the turn of the millennium, reaching two consecutive Champions League finals in 2000 and 2001 and winning La Liga twice. Mestalla is one of Spain's most intimidating atmospheres.
teams.fanGuide: Mestalla's steep, close stands create a cauldron effect that has rattled visiting Champions League contenders for decades. The club's financial troubles contrast with a proud history.

teams.name: Osasuna

players.name: Vinícius Júnior
players.quickFact: Flamengo sold him to Madrid as a teenager — he later scored the decisive goal in a Paris Champions League final.
players.playingStyle: Explosive dribbler who terrorizes full-backs, draws fouls, and finishes in clutch moments.
quizHints: Brazilian left winger who wears the famous number 7 in white at the Bernabéu.
Became a leading voice against repeated racist abuse in Spanish stadiums.
2022 final winner against the club in all-red from Merseyside.

players.name: Jude Bellingham
players.quickFact: Became Birmingham City’s youngest-ever first-team player at 16 years and 38 days.
players.playingStyle: Complete midfielder who arrives in the box, presses aggressively, and leads with charisma.
quizHints: Midlands-born England star who left for Germany before crossing to Spain.
Knee-slide celebration copied by teammates after big goals in white.
Wore Dortmund’s number 22 before starring in Madrid’s number 5 shirt.

players.name: Federico Valverde
players.quickFact: Once played right-back in a Clásico to mark Barcelona’s star winger — coaches trusted his engine over his natural role.
players.playingStyle: Engine midfielder with lung-busting runs, long shots, and tactical discipline on the wing or center.
quizHints: Uruguayan runner with a bleached-blond look in Madrid white.
Peñarol talent signed young by the club with the most European Cups.
Long-range shot in a 2022 Champions League tie injured Liverpool’s keeper.

players.name: Thibaut Courtois
players.quickFact: At roughly 6’7”, he is among the tallest regular starters in elite European football.
players.playingStyle: Giant frame keeper who excels one-on-one and commands the penalty area on crosses.
quizHints: Belgian keeper who represented Atlético, Chelsea, and Real Madrid.
Genk youth product before La Liga and Premier League spells.
Man of the Match in a Paris Champions League final with late saves.

players.name: Robert Lewandowski
players.quickFact: Five goals in nine minutes vs Wolfsburg in 2015 remains one of the Bundesliga’s most famous individual explosions.
players.playingStyle: Complete number 9 with elite movement, link play, and both-footed finishing.
quizHints: Poland’s all-time leading men’s international goalscorer.
Dortmund’s yellow-and-black number 9 before Bayern records and Barça stripes.
Also played volleyball competitively as a teenager before committing to football.

players.name: Pedri
players.quickFact: Stacked club, national team, and Olympics minutes as a teenager in 2021 — sparking talk about burnout before he turned 19.
players.playingStyle: La Masia technician who glides past presses with body feints and progressive passing.
quizHints: Canary Islands-born La Masia midfielder with a low center of gravity.
Las Palmas loanee who became Euro 2020 Young Player of the Tournament.
Often paired in midfield with a younger teammate known for fiery tackles.

players.name: Gavi
players.quickFact: Made his Spain senior debut at 17 — among the youngest in the men’s team in the modern era.
players.playingStyle: Fiery ball-winner with flair dribbles and aggressive personality on the pitch.
quizHints: La Masia graduate whose first name is Pablo but everyone uses a four-letter nickname.
Long hair and aggressive challenges mark his style beside Pedri.
Serious knee surgery interrupted his rapid rise in blaugrana stripes.

players.name: Raphinha
players.quickFact: Left Leeds for Barcelona in 2022 — one of several Brazilians who sharpened their game in England before La Liga.
players.playingStyle: Direct Brazilian winger with tricks, cutbacks, and relentless work off the ball.
quizHints: Brazilian winger who wore white at Elland Road before Camp Nou.
Breakout in Portugal at Sporting before France at Rennes.
Direct dribbler often stationed on Barça’s right in a trident attack.

players.name: Marc-André ter Stegen
players.quickFact: Germany’s number-one debate often pits him against Bayern’s long-serving keeper — both rose through Bundesliga rivals.
players.playingStyle: Sweeper-keeper with reflex saves and excellent distribution to start buildup.
quizHints: Mönchengladbach “Foals” graduate who joined Barça in 2014.
Sweeper-keeper who starts attacks with short passes under pressure.
Catalan club’s long-term number one while Neuer ruled for Die Mannschaft.

players.name: Carles Aleñá
players.quickFact: Spanish central midfielder currently with Alavés in La Liga.
players.playingStyle: Technical central midfielder who distributes from deep and carries the ball forward.
quizHints: Plays for Alavés in La Liga.
Central midfielder.
Spanish.

players.name: Carlos Protesoni
players.quickFact: Uruguayan defensive midfielder playing for Alavés in La Liga.
players.playingStyle: Physical defensive midfielder who breaks up play and protects the defence.
quizHints: Plays for Alavés.
Defensive midfielder.
Uruguayan (Uruguay).

players.name: Jonny Otto
players.quickFact: Spanish right-back in Alavés’ La Liga squad.
players.playingStyle: Defensive-minded right-back who stays compact in a back four.
quizHints: Plays for Alavés.
Right-back / defender.
Spanish.

players.name: Adama Boiro
players.quickFact: Spain left-back who broke through at Athletic Club as a young overlapping defender with pace and left-footed delivery.
players.playingStyle: Athletic left-back who flies forward, delivers crosses, and recovers quickly to defend one-on-ones.
quizHints: Spain youth international left-back at Athletic Club.
Young attacking full-back known for speed and overlapping runs.
Defender whose first name is Adama and surname is Boiro.

players.name: Aitor Paredes
players.quickFact: Spain centre-back who rose through Athletic Club’s academy and became a Basque-side defensive leader with aerial strength.
players.playingStyle: Dominant centre-back who wins headers, blocks shots, and organizes the line with clear communication.
quizHints: Spain international centre-back from Athletic Club’s academy system.
Strong aerial defender associated with the Basque Country club.
Centre-back whose first name is Aitor and surname is Paredes.

players.name: Álex Berenguer
players.quickFact: Spain winger who became an Athletic Club mainstay with direct dribbling, work rate, and goals from the Basque side’s left flank.
players.playingStyle: Hard-running winger who takes on defenders, works defensively, and finishes with power when driving into the box.
quizHints: Spain international winger associated with Athletic Club in the Basque Country.
Direct wide player known for dribbling, pressing, and cutting inside to shoot.
Attacker whose first name is Álex and surname is Berenguer.

players.name: Gorka Guruzeta
players.quickFact: Spain striker who rose through lower Spanish football before becoming an Athletic Club centre-forward in La Liga.
players.playingStyle: Target-style forward who links play with his back to goal, presses aggressively, and finishes close-range chances.
quizHints: Athletic Club forward who plays in the Basque club’s red-and-white stripes.
Spain striker known for hold-up play and movement in the box.
Athletic attacker whose first name is Gorka and surname is Guruzeta.

players.name: Iñaki Williams
players.quickFact: Athletic Club forward born in Bilbao who became a Spain and Ghana dual-national story on the wing.
players.playingStyle: Direct wide forward who uses pace to stretch defenses, attack channels, and press relentlessly off the ball.
quizHints: Came through Athletic’s academy and stayed loyal to the Basque club.
Known for explosive sprints and power on the right side of attack.
Brother Nico also plays professionally; this Iñaki wears red and white stripes.

players.name: Oihan Sancet
players.quickFact: Spain attacking midfielder who became an Athletic Club fan favourite with goals, dribbling, and creativity from the number ten role.
players.playingStyle: Creative playmaker who receives between lines, turns defenders, and finishes with clever shots and passes.
quizHints: Spain international attacking midfielder at Athletic Club in the Basque Country.
Technical number ten known for dribbling and shooting from midfield.
Midfielder whose first name is Oihan and surname is Sancet.

players.name: Yuri Berchiche
players.quickFact: Spain left-back whose long Athletic Club career made him a Basque-side institution with attacking overlap and experience.
players.playingStyle: Experienced left-back who pushes high, crosses with power, and defends the flank with tough one-on-one work.
quizHints: Spain international left-back associated with Athletic Club in the Basque Country.
Veteran full-back known for stamina and driven left-footed delivery.
Defender whose first name is Yuri and surname is Berchiche.

players.name: Ademola Lookman
players.quickFact: Nigeria forward whose dribbling and final-third creativity shone at Atalanta before Atlético Madrid added him in La Liga.
players.playingStyle: Skillful forward who isolates defenders, combines in tight spaces, and creates chances with quick feet in the box.
quizHints: Nigeria international forward who starred in Italy with Atalanta.
Direct wide player known for dribbling and penalty-area combinations.
Atlético Madrid attacker whose first name is Ademola and surname is Lookman.

players.name: Alexander Sørloth
players.quickFact: Norway striker who scored regularly for Real Sociedad and RB Leipzig before becoming a La Liga forward at Atlético Madrid.
players.playingStyle: Mobile striker who attacks channels, holds up play, and finishes with power from inside the box.
quizHints: Norway centre-forward in red who plays for Atlético Madrid in La Liga.
Tall striker — not Erling Haaland at Manchester City.
Alexander Sørloth known for runs in behind and left-footed finishes.

players.name: Antoine Griezmann
players.quickFact: France forward and 2018 World Cup winner who became Atlético Madrid’s all-time leading scorer.
players.playingStyle: Intelligent second striker who drops between lines, presses hard, and creates chances with subtle passes.
quizHints: Made his name in Spain after developing at Real Sociedad.
World Cup winner who links midfield and attack for France.
Left-footed Atlético Madrid forward known for clever movement.

players.name: Jan Oblak
players.quickFact: Slovenia goalkeeper whose reflex saves and consistency made him Atlético Madrid’s long-term number one.
players.playingStyle: Elite shot-stopper who stays composed, commands the box, and rarely needs dramatic dives to make saves.
quizHints: Slovenia international keeper who won multiple La Liga titles with Atlético.
Famous for calm positioning and athletic one-on-one saves.
Atlético Madrid goalkeeper whose first name is Jan and surname is Oblak.

players.name: Marcos Llorente
players.quickFact: Spain midfielder whose engine and versatility let him play right-back or central midfield for Atlético Madrid after Real Madrid youth years.
players.playingStyle: High-energy runner who defends wide areas, carries the ball forward, and supports attacks with late surges.
quizHints: Spain international who came through Real Madrid’s academy.
Known for covering huge distances and playing multiple midfield or wide roles.
Atlético Madrid player whose first name is Marcos and surname is Llorente.

players.name: Robin Le Normand
players.quickFact: Spain centre-back born in France who became a La Roja Euro 2024 starter after establishing himself at Real Sociedad and Atlético Madrid.
players.playingStyle: Physical centre-back who defends the box, wins duels, and plays simple passes to midfield.
quizHints: Spain centre-back in red who won Euro 2024.
Former Real Sociedad pillar now at Atlético Madrid.
Robin Le Normand — France-born but plays for La Roja.

players.name: Thiago Almada
players.quickFact: Argentina playmaker who won the World Cup in 2022 and became known for dribbling, set pieces, and creativity from midfield.
players.playingStyle: Creative midfielder who drifts wide, beats defenders with quick feet, and supplies incisive passes and set-piece delivery.
quizHints: Argentina international who won the 2022 World Cup.
Left-footed playmaker who excels at free kicks and close control.
Midfielder whose first name is Thiago and surname is Almada.

players.name: Alejandro Balde
players.quickFact: Spain left-back whose explosive pace at Barcelona made him a La Liga attacking full-back with end-to-end runs.
players.playingStyle: Lightning-quick left-back who flies forward, delivers crosses, and recovers with elite speed.
quizHints: Spain international left-back from Barcelona’s academy system.
Very fast full-back known for overlapping runs and recovery pace.
Defender whose first name is Alejandro and surname is Balde.

players.name: Frenkie de Jong
players.quickFact: Netherlands midfielder who starred at Ajax and Barcelona as a press-resistant pivot with dribbling and passing range.
players.playingStyle: Elegant midfielder who carries the ball past presses, circulates play, and sets tempo with vision.
quizHints: Netherlands international midfielder long associated with Barcelona.
Press-resistant pivot known for dribbling out of tight spaces.
Midfielder whose first name is Frenkie and surname is de Jong.

players.name: Pau Cubarsí
players.quickFact: Spain centre-back who broke through as a teenager at Barcelona with composure beyond his years in La Liga.
players.playingStyle: Composed young centre-back who reads play early, steps out with the ball, and defends with timing.
quizHints: Spain international centre-back who broke through young at Barcelona.
Teenage defender known for calm defending and passing from the back.
Centre-back whose first name is Pau and surname is Cubarsí.

players.name: Ronald Araujo
players.quickFact: Uruguay centre-back who became a Barcelona regular and La Celeste mainstay with pace, power, and recovery defending.
players.playingStyle: Athletic centre-back who steps out to defend space, wins duels, and plays simple passes from the back.
quizHints: Uruguay centre-back in sky blue who plays for FC Barcelona.
Powerful defender — not José María Giménez at Atlético Madrid.
Ronald Araujo known for recovery speed and duels in the channel.

players.name: Wojciech Szczesny
players.quickFact: Poland goalkeeper with long spells at Arsenal and Juventus who returned to Barcelona as senior national-team cover.
players.playingStyle: Traditional goalkeeper who commands the box, spreads on one-v-ones, and distributes with either foot.
quizHints: Poland goalkeeper in white and red — not Kamil Grabara at Bournemouth.
Former Juventus and Arsenal no. 1 before Barcelona.
Tall shot-stopper named Wojciech with the diacritic on the c.

players.name: Borja Iglesias
players.quickFact: Spain striker nicknamed the Panda whose finishing and penalty confidence made him a La Liga mainstay at Betis and Celta.
players.playingStyle: Classic number nine who holds up play, finishes inside the box, and relies on timing and composure over speed.
quizHints: Celebrates with a panda dance that became his trademark.
Strong penalty taker and poacher who has played for Real Betis.
Celta Vigo centre-forward whose first name is Borja.

players.name: Ilaix Moriba
players.quickFact: Guinea midfielder who emerged at Barcelona’s academy and later became a Celta Vigo midfield option in La Liga.
players.playingStyle: Strong central midfielder who carries through contact, breaks lines with dribbles, and shields play with physical presence.
quizHints: Guinea international midfielder with Barcelona academy roots.
Powerful ball-carrier who can play central midfield in La Liga.
Celta Vigo midfielder whose first name is Ilaix and surname is Moriba.

players.name: Ionuț Radu
players.quickFact: Romania goalkeeper who came through Inter’s system and became a La Liga starter with Celta Vigo after Italian loan spells.
players.playingStyle: Reactive goalkeeper who commands the area, makes reflex saves, and distributes accurately to start build-up play.
quizHints: Romania international goalkeeper with Inter Milan in his career path.
Shot-stopper who played in Serie A before La Liga with Celta.
Goalkeeper whose first name is Ionuț and surname is Radu.

players.name: Marcos Alonso
players.quickFact: Spain left-back who won the Champions League with Chelsea and later brought experience to Celta Vigo with goals and crossing.
players.playingStyle: Attacking left-back who overlaps, shoots from distance, and delivers dangerous balls into the box from wide areas.
quizHints: Spain international left-back who won the Champions League with Chelsea.
Attacking full-back known for left-footed shooting and aerial threat from set pieces.
Defender whose first name is Marcos and surname is Alonso.

players.name: Sergio Carreira
players.quickFact: Spain right-back who developed at Celta Vigo as an overlapping full-back with energy and delivery from wide areas.
players.playingStyle: Attacking right-back who pushes high, whips in crosses, and recovers quickly to defend one-on-ones.
quizHints: Spain youth international full-back based at Celta Vigo.
Right-sided defender known for stamina and crossing from the flank.
Full-back whose first name is Sergio and surname is Carreira.

players.name: Federico Redondo
players.quickFact: Argentine defensive midfielder currently with Elche in La Liga.
players.playingStyle: Tall defensive midfielder who screens the defence and distributes simply.
quizHints: Plays for Elche.
Defensive midfielder.
Argentine (Argentina).

players.name: Germán Valera
players.quickFact: Spanish winger in Elche’s La Liga squad.
players.playingStyle: Left-sided winger who dribbles at defenders and crosses into the box.
quizHints: Plays for Elche.
Left winger / attacker.
Spanish.

players.name: Rafa Mir
players.quickFact: Spanish centre-forward in Elche’s La Liga squad.
players.playingStyle: Target-style centre-forward who holds up play and finishes in the box.
quizHints: Plays for Elche in La Liga.
Centre-forward.
Spanish.

players.name: Fernando Calero
players.quickFact: Spanish centre-back for Espanyol in La Liga.
players.playingStyle: Composed centre-back who reads the game and steps out to intercept.
quizHints: Plays for Espanyol.
Centre-back / defender.
Spanish.

players.name: Javi Puado
players.quickFact: Spanish winger in Espanyol’s La Liga squad.
players.playingStyle: Direct winger who cuts inside from the left and looks to create chances.
quizHints: Plays for Espanyol in La Liga.
Left winger / attacker.
Spanish.

players.name: Jofre Carreras
players.quickFact: Spanish right winger in Espanyol’s La Liga squad.
players.playingStyle: Pacey wide attacker who stretches defences on the right flank.
quizHints: Plays for Espanyol in La Liga.
Right winger / attacker.
Spanish.

players.name: David Soria
players.quickFact: Spanish goalkeeper for Getafe in La Liga.
players.playingStyle: Reliable goalkeeper who organises his defence and commands the penalty area.
quizHints: Plays for Getafe in La Liga.
Goalkeeper.
Spanish.

players.name: Diego Rico
players.quickFact: Spanish left-back in Getafe’s La Liga squad.
players.playingStyle: Attacking left-back who overlaps and delivers crosses from wide areas.
quizHints: Plays for Getafe.
Left-back / defender.
Spanish.

players.name: Juanmi
players.quickFact: Spanish attacker currently in Getafe’s La Liga squad.
players.playingStyle: Mobile forward who drifts wide, links play, and finishes inside the box.
quizHints: Plays for Getafe.

Spanish.

players.name: Abel Ruiz
players.quickFact: Spanish centre-forward playing for Girona in La Liga.
players.playingStyle: Mobile centre-forward who presses from the front and links midfield to attack.
quizHints: Plays for Girona.
Centre-forward.
Spanish.

players.quickFact: Uruguayan striker in Girona’s La Liga squad.
players.playingStyle: Clinical centre-forward who finds space in the box and finishes with both feet.

players.playingStyle: Left-footed attacking midfielder who combines dribbling with through-balls and shots.

Attacking midfielder / winger.
French (France).

players.quickFact: Spanish centre-forward playing for Levante in La Liga.

Centre-forward.

players.name: Jeremy Toljan
players.quickFact: German right-back playing for Levante in La Liga.
players.playingStyle: Energetic right-back who joins attacks and recovers quickly in transition.
quizHints: Plays for Levante.
Right-back / defender.
German (Germany).
